stem: Let \( f(x) = [[a]]x + ([[b]]) \). For what value of \( x \) is \( f(x) = [[c]] \)?
distractor_rationales: This subtracts \( [[b]] \) but forgets to divide by the coefficient \( [[a]] \).
This negates the correct value (a sign slip when isolating \( x \)).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

solution: By definition, \( f^{-1}(y) = x \) exactly when \( f(x) = y \). Since \( f([[x3]]) = [[y3]] \), it follows directly that \( f^{-1}([[y3]]) = [[x3]] \) — no formula for \( f \) is needed, just matching the given pair.
distractor_rationales: This picks the input from a different (mismatched) pair in the table.

stem: Let \( f \) be a one-to-one function with \( f([[a]]) = [[b]] \), and let \( f^{-1} \) be its inverse. What is \( f^{-1}([[b]]) + f\big(f^{-1}([[b]])\big) \)?
solution: By definition of the inverse, \( f^{-1}([[b]]) = [[a]] \) since \( f([[a]]) = [[b]] \). Then \( f\big(f^{-1}([[b]])\big) = f([[a]]) = [[b]] \). Adding these: \( [[a]] + [[b]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This doubles \( f^{-1}([[b]]) \) instead of adding it to \( f(f^{-1}([[b]])) \).
This doubles \( f(f^{-1}([[b]])) \) instead of adding the two (equal-looking but conceptually different) quantities.
This subtracts the two quantities instead of adding them.
This subtracts in the opposite order.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: A linear function \( f \) satisfies \( f([[p]]) = [[A]] \) and \( f([[q]]) = [[B]] \). For what value of \( x \) is \( f(x) = [[V]] \)? (You do not need to find a formula for \( f \).)
solution: Since \( f \) is linear, equal steps in \( x \) between \( [[p]] \) and \( [[q]] \) correspond to proportional steps in output between \( [[A]] \) and \( [[B]] \). Here \( [[V]] \) is reached at \( [[t]] \) times the step from \( [[p]] \) to \( [[q]] \), so \( x = [[p]] + [[t]]([[q]]-[[p]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This measures the same proportional step from \( [[q]] \) instead of from \( [[p]] \).
This steps in the wrong direction, subtracting the scaled step instead of adding it.
This divides by the scale factor \( [[t]] \) instead of multiplying by it.
This reports the scale factor itself instead of the resulting \( x \)-value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Let \( g \) be the inverse of a one-to-one function \( f \), with \( g([[v1]]) = [[w1]] \) and \( g([[v2]]) = [[w2]] \). What is \( f([[w1]]) + f([[w2]]) \)?
distractor_rationales: This adds the inverse's outputs \( g([[v1]]) \) and \( g([[v2]]) \) instead of \( f \) applied to them.
This subtracts the two values instead of adding them.
This subtracts in the opposite order.
This subtracts the inverse's outputs instead of adding the corresponding \( f \)-values.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
